name: Crawfish Casserole
recipeIngredient: St. Romain white wine (1 bottle, approximately 750 ml)
10 3/4 oz can cream of mushroom soup
1 tablespoon garlic powder, or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a double broiler, melt the stick of butter and add the cubed cream cheese. Stir over low heat until the cheese is melted.
2. In a separate skillet, melt 2 tablespoons margarine. Sauté the crawfish tails, onions, bell pepper, and mushrooms until the seasonings are limp.
3. Add the mushroom soup and cooked rice to the skillet. Stir in garlic powder and red pepper flakes.
4. Mix in the cream cheese and melted margarine mixture with the crawfish mixture. Stir well and adjust seasoning if needed.
5. Grease a casserole dish and pour in the mixture. Top with grated Velvetta cheese and fried onion rings.
6. Bake at 350°F for 30 minutes, or until bubbly. Serve 8 to 10 people.
7. This recipe freezes well and can be made up to a day before serving. Shrimp can also be substituted for the crawfish.
